review:
SUMMARY: This review covers five confirmed findings related to resource copying, fallback precedence, a typo, silent exception handling, and an unused import in the new classpath-based multi-release compilation feature. Following the points, four hedged questions are raised for the author regarding backward compatibility, test coverage, toolchain resolution, and structural refactoring edge cases.

POINTS:
- [tycho-compiler-plugin/src/main/java/org/eclipse/tycho/compiler/AbstractOsgiCompilerMojo.java:415] Classpath-based multi-release compilation does not call doCopyResources() after super.execute(), so non-Java resource files (XML, properties, etc.) from version-specific source folders will not be copied to META-INF/versions/N/, unlike the base compilation path which does call doCopyResources() on line 386.
- [tycho-compiler-plugin/src/main/java/org/eclipse/tycho/compiler/AbstractOsgiCompilerMojo.java:403-405] When .classpath has source folder entries with release attributes, the classpath-based approach takes precedence and completely ignores releases discovered from META-INF/versions/ directories via getMultiReleases(). If a user has both classpath entries with release="9" and a META-INF/versions/11/ directory, release 11 sources would be silently skipped without warning.
- [demo/multi-release-jar-classpath/src/tycho/mr/example/Main.java:9] Typo in demo Main.java: "at laest one file" should be "at least one file".
- [tycho-metadata-model/src/main/java/org/eclipse/tycho/model/classpath/SourceFolderClasspathEntry.java:39-41] In getMultiReleaseVersion(), NumberFormatException for invalid release values is silently caught and ignored. A non-numeric value like "latest" or a typo like "nine" would be silently ignored with no warning logged, which could make debugging confusing if a user accidentally misspells the release value.
- [demo/multi-release-jar-classpath/src9/tycho/mr/example/HttpClient.java:7] Unused import in demo src9/tycho/mr/example/HttpClient.java: import org.apache.commons.io.IOUtils; is present but not used - the src9 version uses stream.readAllBytes() instead. This is a cleanup issue, not a functional bug.

OPEN QUESTIONS:
- Regarding backward compatibility, when .classpath has source entries but none have release attributes, the code falls back to the directory-based approach. Could you confirm whether this fallback correctly handles cases where META-INF/versions/ directories exist alongside classpath entries without release attributes, ensuring the old behavior isn't inadvertently altered?
- The integration test testTychoMultiReleaseClasspathDemo() currently only verifies that the build completes without errors. Would it be beneficial to add assertions that validate the resulting JAR actually contains the expected META-INF/versions/N/ structure and compiled classes, to prevent incorrect classpath attribute handling from passing silently?
- The demo's .classpath specifies JavaSE-1.8 for the base JRE, but the compilation uses --release 9 and --release 11. Could you clarify whether the current toolchain resolution setup guarantees that a JDK capable of --release 11 will be available, or if this might require explicit toolchain configuration in practice?
- The refactoring moves the getMultiReleases() call into a conditional else branch and adjusts the currentSourceRoots check. Have you verified that this structural change doesn't introduce edge cases where both the classpath-based and directory-based approaches might need to apply simultaneously, or where the fallback behavior could diverge from the original implementation?
